intro: Apium graveolens, also known as wild celery, is a plant-based ingredient used in various health remedies, including the production of syrup. The syrup made from wild celery is said to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving digestion. Traditionally, apium graveolens syrup has been used to treat various ailments, such as stomach issues, and as a natural remedy for stress relief.

recipe: To make apium graveolens syrup for medicinal use, the roots and rhizomes of Apium graveolens, also known as celery seed, should be harvested and dried to create a herbal extract. This extract can be mixed with a sugar syrup, typically made from 1 part herbal extract to 3-5 parts sugar and water, and then simmered until the sugar dissolves. Once the syrup has cooled, it can be strained and bottled for medicinal use, following proper dosing and safety guidelines.
